race_name: L'Étape Bulgaria by Tour de France - Peshtera

top_categories.workout_context: L'Étape Bulgaria by Tour de France - Peshtera's mountain passes forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 63 miles.
Race-pace efforts that mimic L'Étape Bulgaria by Tour de France - Peshtera's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before July.
8,202ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
63 miles on mountain passes burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
129ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At L'Étape Bulgaria by Tour de France - Peshtera, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At L'Étape Bulgaria by Tour de France - Peshtera, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
8,202ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of L'Étape Bulgaria by Tour de France - Peshtera. Train it until it feels boring.
Base fitness for L'Étape Bulgaria by Tour de France - Peshtera. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mountain passes. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Low-cadence force work for 8,202ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.

race_overlay.heat: July in Peshtera, Bulgaria can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before L'Étape Bulgaria by Tour de France - Peshtera. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for L'Étape Bulgaria by Tour de France - Peshtera’s 63 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at L'Étape Bulgaria by Tour de France - Peshtera. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Highly technical terrain at L'Étape Bulgaria by Tour de France - Peshtera demands practice on similar surfaces. Ride mountain passes at race-day cadence weekly. Practice cornering, descending, and power delivery on unstable surfaces. Dial in tire pressure before race week — 5 PSI wrong costs you 15+ minutes over 63 miles.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Gravel Specific, Race Simulation, and VO2max to prepare you for 63 miles of mountain passes in Peshtera, Bulgaria.
